description: Recent Zenith release notes covering merged core stability and editor-tooling work.

intro.title: MERGED WORK. PUBLIC RECORD.
intro.description: The public site now surfaces the recent framework and editor batches directly. Install commands stay on the current train line, while the docs-side changelog records the merged core and language-tooling work through v0.6.18.

releases.description: Core stability fixes, server/client routing hardening, and compiler-backed editor tooling now land together on one coherent release train.

items.description: Slot-scope owner, marker namespace, SVG class binding, and CLI expression rewrite regressions were fixed without site-level workarounds.
Server-authoritative routing, client fetch-before-commit navigation, lifecycle hooks, and the narrow document-transition prototype now share one documented contract.
Structured compiler diagnostics, first ZEN-DOM quick fixes, canonical hover/completion parity, and packaged editor verification all align to the same 0.6.18 train.

releases.description: Release metadata hardening kept the train aligned so publish and CI preflight no longer fail on already-bootstrapped platform package names.

items.description: Train metadata checks now tolerate the expected npm tag state for existing platform packages.
Release-train alignment remains enforced through the shared package version gate.
Bundler, compiler, and package metadata now have one release line to promote instead of split train identities.

releases.description: The monorepo gained the canonical language package and language server as first-class release-train packages.

items.description: Zenith now ships one canonical grammar, snippet set, and language-server path for editor support.
CI coverage landed for grammar embedding, hover docs, completions, and DOM lint diagnostics.

releases.description: The documentation surface locked the internal Tailwind and dev CSS contracts that the public site still follows today.

items.description: Tailwind v4 moved into the built-in Zenith pipeline for both dev and build.
The docs locked `/__zenith_dev/styles.css` as the canonical dev CSS contract.
